title: angelica ubadakensis
intro: Angelica ubadakensis, a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, is native to Japan and is also known as Ube or Angelica keiskei. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Angelica ubadakensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
